weather_report:
Night has settled over Subiaco with a fine, unhurried rain beginning to tap at roofs and leaves. The sky is sealed in cloud, streetlights bloom with soft halos, and edges blur to a gentle hush. There’s no sunshine left to give the evening any gleam, and the UV index lies essentially asleep until morning. Expect light showers on and off through late evening and into the small hours, more a silky veil than a downpour, leaving pavements slick and reflective.

It’s a night for a light waterproof layer and closed shoes, nothing bulky, just enough to keep the drizzle polite. If you must head out, walk under awnings and take your time; if you’re staying in, tend to the small things that like rain’s company: rinse a few dishes, set up tomorrow’s bag, leaf through a chapter, or give the balcony plants a brief look while the air smells rinsed and clean. The weather isn’t harsh, only quietly insistent, and with a little care it makes an ordinary late night feel considered and calm.
